name: Camp Upton
description: The Camp Upton was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, housing and training the 77th Division for Doughboy basic training for northeastern conscripts. The camp was built using emergency construction contracts, with wooden barracks, mess halls, and YMCA huts erected in a matter of weeks to accommodate the flood of conscripts called up under the Selective Service Act of 1917. Troops drilled with Springfield rifles and bayonets on open parade grounds and maneuver fields before deploying to France as part of the American Expeditionary Forces. Much of the original cantonment footprint is now on public or state recreational land. Metal detectorists working the old parade grounds and firing lines have recovered 1918 wheat-cent pennies, brass collar insignia, Victory Medal ribbon hardware, and cotton webbing grommets.
source: NARA Record Group 391 / American Expeditionary Forces Records

description: The Camp Devens was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, housing and training the 76th Division for infantry training and officer-candidate courses. source: NARA Record Group 391 / American Expeditionary Forces Records

description: The Camp Forrest (GA, WWI) was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, housing and training the Depot Brigade for replacement infantry training in north Georgia. The camp was built using emergency construction contracts, with wooden barracks, mess halls, and YMCA huts erected in a matter of weeks to accommodate the flood of conscripts called up under the Selective Service Act of 1917. Troops drilled with Springfield rifles and bayonets on open parade grounds and maneuver fields before deploying to France as part of the American Expeditionary Forces. Much of the original cantonment footprint is now on public or state recreational land. Metal detectorists working the old parade grounds and firing lines have recovered Springfield .30-06 brass, collar insignia, and 1917 Lincoln pennies.
source: NARA Record Group 391 / American Expeditionary Forces Records

name: Camp Jackson Rifle Range (SC)
description: The Camp Jackson Rifle Range (SC) was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, hosting the 81st Division for Wildcat Division weapons qualification at Columbia cantonment. The camp was built using emergency construction contracts, with wooden barracks, mess halls, and YMCA huts erected in weeks to accommodate conscripts called up under the Selective Service Act of 1917. Troops drilled with Springfield rifles and bayonets on open parade grounds before deploying to France as part of the American Expeditionary Forces. Much of the original cantonment footprint is now on public or state recreational land. Metal detectorists working the old parade grounds and firing lines have recovered Springfield .30-06 brass, collar disc 'US', and 1918 wheat pennies.
source: NARA Record Group 391 / American Expeditionary Forces Records

name: Camp Gordon Signals Range (GA)
description: The Camp Gordon Signals Range (GA) was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, hosting the Signal Corps Training for signal corps radio and telegraph training at Fort Gordon cantonment. The camp was built using emergency construction contracts, with wooden barracks, mess halls, and YMCA huts erected in weeks to accommodate conscripts called up under the Selective Service Act of 1917. Troops drilled with Springfield rifles and bayonets on open parade grounds before deploying to France as part of the American Expeditionary Forces. Much of the original cantonment footprint is now on public or state recreational land. Metal detectorists working the old parade grounds and firing lines have recovered wire reel hardware, .30-06 brass, and 1917-dated Lincoln cents.
source: NARA Record Group 391 / American Expeditionary Forces Records

name: Plattsburg Barracks Training Camp (NY)
description: The Plattsburg Barracks Training Camp (NY) was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, hosting the Reserve Officers Training Camp for civilian preparedness 'Plattsburg Movement' officer training. Troops drilled with Springfield rifles and bayonets on open parade grounds before deploying to France as part of the American Expeditionary Forces. Much of the original cantonment footprint is now on public or state recreational land. Metal detectorists working the old parade grounds and firing lines have recovered Springfield .30-06 brass, officer insignia bar, and 1917-dated Barber dimes.
source: NARA Record Group 391 / American Expeditionary Forces Records

name: Fort Sheridan Training Area (IL)
description: The Fort Sheridan Training Area (IL) was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, hosting the Reserve Officers Training for Chicago-area reserve officer candidate training. name: Camp Greenleaf Outlying Range (GA)
description: The Camp Greenleaf Outlying Range (GA) was established in 1917 as part of the Army's National Army cantonment expansion program, hosting the Medical Officers for additional field exercises for medical officer candidates. The camp was built using emergency construction contracts, with wooden barracks, mess halls, and YMCA huts erected in weeks to accommodate conscripts called up under the Selective Service Act of 1917. Troops drilled with Springfield rifles and bayonets on open parade grounds before deploying to France as part of the American Expeditionary Forces. source: NARA Record Group 391 / American Expeditionary Forces Records
